 {"id":"bd-36x8","title":"CRITICAL: E2E Hook Context Non-Interference Test","description":"## THE MOST CRITICAL TEST FOR THIS ENTIRE INTEGRATION

This test verifies that AI coding agents (the PRIMARY users of RCH) are completely unaffected by rich_rust integration. ANY regression here is a BLOCKER.

### What This Tests

When rch is invoked via Claude Code PreToolUse hook:
1. stdout JSON responses are BYTE-FOR-BYTE identical to pre-integration
2. NO rich output appears on stdout (zero bytes of ANSI codes)
3. Exit codes are EXACTLY preserved (0, 1, 101, 128+N)
4. Timing is not degraded (hook classification still <1ms)
5. stderr can have rich output but ONLY if not captured by agent

### Test Implementation

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# scripts/test_hook_non_interference.sh
set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
PROJECT_ROOT="$(dirname "$SCRIPT_DIR")"
TEST_LOG="$PROJECT_ROOT/target/test_hook_interference.log"

log() { echo "[$(date +%H:%M:%S)] $*" | tee -a "$TEST_LOG"; }
pass() { log "✓ PASS: $*"; }
fail() { log "✗ FAIL: $*"; exit 1; }

# Build rch with rich-ui enabled
log "Building rch with rich-ui feature..."
cargo build -p rch --features rich-ui --release

RCH="$PROJECT_ROOT/target/release/rch"

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# TEST 1: Hook JSON Response Integrity
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
log "TEST 1: Hook JSON Response Integrity"

# Simulate hook input (Tier-0 passthrough: 'echo')
HOOK_INPUT='{"tool":"Bash","input":{"command":"echo hello"}}'

# Capture stdout and stderr separately
STDOUT_FILE="$(mktemp)"
STDERR_FILE="$(mktemp)"
trap "rm -f $STDOUT_FILE $STDERR_FILE" EXIT

echo "$HOOK_INPUT" | "$RCH" hook --stdin > "$STDOUT_FILE" 2> "$STDERR_FILE"

# Verify stdout is valid JSON
if ! jq -e . "$STDOUT_FILE" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    fail "stdout is not valid JSON"
fi

# Verify stdout contains NO ANSI escape codes
if grep -qP '\x1b\[' "$STDOUT_FILE"; then
    fail "stdout contains ANSI escape codes!"
fi

# Verify stdout has expected structure
if ! jq -e '.result' "$STDOUT_FILE" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    fail "stdout missing .result field"
fi

pass "Hook JSON response is valid and clean"

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# TEST 2: Hook Exit Codes Preserved  
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
log "TEST 2: Hook Exit Codes Preserved"

# Test allow response (exit 0)
ALLOW_INPUT='{"tool":"Bash","input":{"command":"echo hello"}}'
echo "$ALLOW_INPUT" | "$RCH" hook --stdin >/dev/null 2>&1
ALLOW_EXIT=$?

if [[ $ALLOW_EXIT -ne 0 ]]; then
    fail "Allow response should exit 0, got $ALLOW_EXIT"
fi
pass "Allow response exits 0"

# Test intercept response (exit 101 is handled by daemon, so test structure)
# ... additional exit code tests ...

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# TEST 3: Hook Classification Timing (<1ms for Tier-0)
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
log "TEST 3: Hook Classification Timing"

# Run 100 hook invocations and measure average
ITERATIONS=100
TIMING_LOG="$(mktemp)"

for i in $(seq 1 $ITERATIONS); do
    START=$(date +%s%N)
    echo '{"tool":"Bash","input":{"command":"echo test"}}' | "$RCH" hook --stdin >/dev/null 2>&1
    END=$(date +%s%N)
    echo $((END - START)) >> "$TIMING_LOG"
done

AVG_NS=$(awk '{ sum += $1 } END { print int(sum/NR) }' "$TIMING_LOG")
AVG_MS=$(echo "scale=2; $AVG_NS / 1000000" | bc)

log "Average hook time: ${AVG_MS}ms over $ITERATIONS iterations"

# Threshold: 5ms (generous for CI variance, real target is <1ms)
if (( AVG_NS > 5000000 )); then
    fail "Hook classification too slow: ${AVG_MS}ms (threshold: 5ms)"
fi
pass "Hook classification timing acceptable: ${AVG_MS}ms"

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# TEST 4: Comparison with rich-ui DISABLED
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
log "TEST 4: Comparison with rich-ui disabled"

# Build without rich-ui
cargo build -p rch --no-default-features --release --target-dir target/no-rich

NO_RICH_RCH="$PROJECT_ROOT/target/no-rich/release/rch"

# Compare outputs byte-for-byte
echo "$HOOK_INPUT" | "$RCH" hook --stdin > /tmp/with_rich.json 2>/dev/null
echo "$HOOK_INPUT" | "$NO_RICH_RCH" hook --stdin > /tmp/no_rich.json 2>/dev/null

if ! diff /tmp/with_rich.json /tmp/no_rich.json; then
    fail "stdout differs between rich-ui enabled/disabled builds!"
fi
pass "stdout identical with/without rich-ui feature"

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# SUMMARY
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
log ""
log "═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════"
log "ALL HOOK NON-INTERFERENCE TESTS PASSED"
log "═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════"
```

### Acceptance Criteria

1. [ ] stdout JSON byte-for-byte identical pre/post integration
2. [ ] Zero ANSI codes in stdout
3. [ ] Exit codes exactly preserved
4. [ ] Hook timing <5ms (target <1ms)
5. [ ] Output identical with/without rich-ui feature
6. [ ] Test runs in CI on every PR

 {"id":"bd-37o8","title":"Phase 5: Progress & Feedback - Real-time Visual Feedback","description":"Add real-time progress visualization for long-running operations. This phase covers: TransferProgress for rsync operations, CompilationProgress for build status, multi-stage pipeline visualization, spinner animations for indeterminate operations, and completion celebrations for successful builds. The focus is on keeping humans informed during the 10-60+ second operations that are common in compilation workflows.

Design principles:
- Progress output to stderr only (never pollute stdout)
- Use inline updates (carriage return) to avoid log spam
- Degrade gracefully: full bar → simple percentage → dots → nothing (based on capability)
- Support both determinate (known total) and indeterminate (spinner) modes
- Cancel-safe: progress display must clean up on Ctrl+C
- Rate-limit updates to max 10/second to avoid terminal flicker","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:09:23.453625673Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T21:09:23.465258906Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-37o8","depends_on_id":"bd-3dv2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:09:23.465174156Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-38kz","title":"Phase 1: Foundation - Infrastructure & Context Detection","description":"# Phase 1: Foundation - Infrastructure & Context Detection

## Overview

This phase establishes the foundational infrastructure for rich_rust integration without changing any existing behavior. By the end of this phase, the codebase will have all necessary modules, types, and utilities in place, but rich output will be opt-in via feature flag (defaulting to OFF during development).

## Why This Phase is Critical

The foundation must be rock-solid because:
1. Every subsequent phase builds on these primitives
2. Context detection determines when rich output is safe to use
3. The theme provides visual consistency across all components
4. The feature flag allows emergency rollback

## Key Deliverables

### 1. Dependency Addition
- Add rich_rust to workspace Cargo.toml
- Add to individual crate Cargo.tomls with appropriate features
- Use path dependency during development, crates.io when published

### 2. UI Module Structure
Create the following directory structure:
```
rch-common/src/ui/
  mod.rs       - Re-exports and public API
  context.rs   - OutputContext enum and detection
  theme.rs     - RchTheme with color constants
  icons.rs     - Unicode icons with ASCII fallbacks

rch/src/ui/
  mod.rs       - Re-exports
  console.rs   - RchConsole wrapper

rchd/src/ui/
  mod.rs       - Re-exports
```

### 3. OutputContext Detection
Implement automatic detection of execution context:
- Hook mode: JSON on stdin, should use NO rich output
- Interactive: TTY on stderr, full rich experience
- Colored: No TTY but FORCE_COLOR set, colors only
- Plain: No TTY, no FORCE_COLOR, plain text
- Machine: --json flag or RCH_JSON env var

### 4. RchConsole Wrapper
A context-aware Console that:
- Respects OutputContext settings
- Provides fallback methods (print_or_plain)
- Exposes is_rich() and is_machine() checks

### 5. RchTheme Constants
Define brand colors and semantic styles:
- Primary/Secondary/Accent colors
- Success/Warning/Error/Info semantic colors
- Worker status colors (Healthy/Degraded/Unreachable/etc)
- Text colors (Muted, Dim)

### 6. Feature Flag
- Add 'rich-ui' feature to Cargo.toml
- Default to ON in final release, OFF during development
- Allow compile-time removal of entire rich_rust dependency

## Technical Considerations

### Performance
- Use once_cell::Lazy for static console instances
- Do NOT import UI modules in hook classification path
- Context detection must be <0.1ms

### Compatibility
- Must work on Linux, macOS, Windows
- Must handle terminals without Unicode support
- Must respect NO_COLOR standard (https://no-color.org/)

### Testing
- Unit tests for each OutputContext variant
- Test NO_COLOR detection
- Test FORCE_COLOR levels (0-3)
- Test TTY vs pipe detection

## Acceptance Criteria

1. [ ] rich_rust compiles as workspace dependency
2. [ ] UI module structure exists in rch-common, rch, rchd
3. [ ] OutputContext::detect() returns correct context
4. [ ] RchTheme colors parse successfully
5. [ ] Feature flag 'rich-ui' toggles compilation
6. [ ] All existing tests still pass
7. [ ] cargo check --all-targets succeeds

## Files to Create/Modify

New files:
- rch-common/src/ui/mod.rs
- rch-common/src/ui/context.rs
- rch-common/src/ui/theme.rs
- rch-common/src/ui/icons.rs
- rch/src/ui/mod.rs
- rch/src/ui/console.rs
- rchd/src/ui/mod.rs

Modified files:
- Cargo.toml (workspace)
- rch/Cargo.toml
- rchd/Cargo.toml
- rch-common/Cargo.toml
- rch/src/lib.rs (add ui module)
- rchd/src/main.rs (add ui module)
- rch-common/src/lib.rs (add ui module)

## Risk Mitigation

If rich_rust causes issues:
1. Feature flag allows quick disable at compile time
2. OutputContext::Hook ensures zero impact on agent protocol
3. - Rate limiter: max 10 updates/second regardless of data rate

- Register signal handler for SIGINT/SIGTERM
- Handle terminal resize (SIGWINCH) gracefully
- Support nested progress contexts (push/pop)

    fn drop(&mut self) {
        self.restore_terminal();
        self.clear_progress_line();
    }
}

 {"id":"bd-3brs","title":"Create RchTheme with color constants and semantic styles","description":"# Create RchTheme with color constants and semantic styles

## Task Description

Create rch-common/src/ui/theme.rs with the RchTheme struct containing all color constants and style generators for consistent visual branding across RCH.

## Background

A unified theme ensures:
1. Visual consistency across all RCH commands
2. Single place to update colors if branding changes
3. Semantic meaning (error = red, success = green)
4. Worker status colors match mental model

## Implementation

### Color Palette

```rust
use rich_rust::prelude::*;

/// RCH brand colors and semantic styles
pub struct RchTheme;

impl RchTheme {
    // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════
    // BRAND COLORS
    // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════

    /// Primary brand color - Purple (compilation/build theme)
    pub const PRIMARY: &'static str = "#7C3AED";

    /// Secondary brand color - Cyan (data/transfer theme)
    pub const SECONDARY: &'static str = "#06B6D4";

    /// Accent color - Amber (highlights, attention)
    pub const ACCENT: &'static str = "#F59E0B";

    // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════
    // SEMANTIC COLORS
    // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════

    /// Success state - Green
    pub const SUCCESS: &'static str = "#10B981";

    /// Warning state - Amber
    pub const WARNING: &'static str = "#F59E0B";

    /// Error state - Red
    pub const ERROR: &'static str = "#EF4444";

    /// Informational - Blue
    pub const INFO: &'static str = "#3B82F6";

    // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════
    // WORKER STATUS COLORS
    // These match the WorkerStatus enum from rch-common/types
    // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════

    /// Worker is healthy and accepting work
    pub const STATUS_HEALTHY: &'static str = "#10B981";     // Green

    /// Worker is degraded (slow, high error rate)
    pub const STATUS_DEGRADED: &'static str = "#F59E0B";    // Amber

    /// Worker is unreachable (connection failed)
    pub const STATUS_UNREACHABLE: &'static str = "#EF4444"; // Red

    /// Worker is draining (no new work)
    pub const STATUS_DRAINING: &'static str = "#8B5CF6";    // Purple

    /// Worker is disabled (admin disabled)
    pub const STATUS_DISABLED: &'static str = "#6B7280";    // Gray

    // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════
    // TEXT COLORS
    // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════

    /// Muted text (secondary information)
    pub const MUTED: &'static str = "#9CA3AF";              // Gray-400

    /// Dim text (tertiary information)
    pub const DIM: &'static str = "#6B7280";                // Gray-500

    /// Bright text (emphasis)
    pub const BRIGHT: &'static str = "#F9FAFB";             // Gray-50

    // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════
    // STYLE GENERATORS
    // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════

    /// Create style for success messages
    pub fn success() -> Style {
        Style::new().color(Color::parse(Self::SUCCESS).unwrap_or_default())
    }

    /// Create style for error messages (bold for emphasis)
    pub fn error() -> Style {
        Style::new()
            .bold()
            .color(Color::parse(Self::ERROR).unwrap_or_default())
    }

    /// Create style for warning messages
    pub fn warning() -> Style {
        Style::new().color(Color::parse(Self::WARNING).unwrap_or_default())
    }

    /// Create style for informational messages
    pub fn info() -> Style {
        Style::new().color(Color::parse(Self::INFO).unwrap_or_default())
    }

    /// Create style for muted/secondary text
    pub fn muted() -> Style {
        Style::new().color(Color::parse(Self::MUTED).unwrap_or_default())
    }

    /// Create style for dim/tertiary text
    pub fn dim() -> Style {
        Style::new()
            .dim()
            .color(Color::parse(Self::DIM).unwrap_or_default())
    }

    /// Create style for primary brand elements
    pub fn primary() -> Style {
        Style::new().color(Color::parse(Self::PRIMARY).unwrap_or_default())
    }

    /// Create style for secondary brand elements
    pub fn secondary() -> Style {
        Style::new().color(Color::parse(Self::SECONDARY).unwrap_or_default())
    }

    /// Create style for worker status based on WorkerStatus enum
    pub fn worker_status(status: &str) -> Style {
        let color = match status.to_lowercase().as_str() {
            "healthy" => Self::STATUS_HEALTHY,
            "degraded" => Self::STATUS_DEGRADED,
            "unreachable" => Self::STATUS_UNREACHABLE,
            "draining" => Self::STATUS_DRAINING,
            "disabled" => Self::STATUS_DISABLED,
            _ => Self::MUTED,
        };
        Style::new().color(Color::parse(color).unwrap_or_default())
    }

    // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════
    // COMPOSITE STYLES
    // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════

    /// Style for table headers
    pub fn table_header() -> Style {
        Style::new()
            .bold()
            .color(Color::parse(Self::BRIGHT).unwrap_or_default())
    }

    /// Style for table borders
    pub fn table_border() -> Style {
        Style::new().color(Color::parse(Self::PRIMARY).unwrap_or_default())
    }

    /// Style for panel titles
    pub fn panel_title() -> Style {
        Style::new()
            .bold()
            .color(Color::parse(Self::SECONDARY).unwrap_or_default())
    }

    /// Style for command/code text
    pub fn code() -> Style {
        Style::new()
            .color(Color::parse(Self::SECONDARY).unwrap_or_default())
    }

    /// Style for paths/filenames
    pub fn path() -> Style {
        Style::new()
            .italic()
            .color(Color::parse(Self::INFO).unwrap_or_default())
    }

    /// Style for numbers/metrics
    pub fn number() -> Style {
        Style::new().color(Color::parse(Self::ACCENT).unwrap_or_default())
    }
}
```

### Integration with WorkerStatus Type

```rust
// This function should be added or the above adapted
// to work with the actual WorkerStatus enum from rch-common/types
use crate::types::WorkerStatus;

impl RchTheme {
    pub fn for_worker_status(status: WorkerStatus) -> Style {
        let color = match status {
            WorkerStatus::Healthy => Self::STATUS_HEALTHY,
            WorkerStatus::Degraded => Self::STATUS_DEGRADED,
            WorkerStatus::Unreachable => Self::STATUS_UNREACHABLE,
            WorkerStatus::Draining => Self::STATUS_DRAINING,
            WorkerStatus::Disabled => Self::STATUS_DISABLED,
        };
        Style::new().color(Color::parse(color).unwrap_or_default())
    }
}
```

## Color Choice Rationale

### Brand Colors
- **Purple (#7C3AED)**: Compilation/build - sophisticated, technical
- **Cyan (#06B6D4)**: Data transfer - clean, digital
- **Amber (#F59E0B)**: Attention - warm, visible

### Semantic Colors
- **Green (#10B981)**: Success - universal meaning
- **Amber (#F59E0B)**: Warning - caution without alarm
- **Red (#EF4444)**: Error - immediate attention
- **Blue (#3B82F6)**: Info - calm, informational

### Worker Status
- **Green**: Healthy - good to go
- **Amber**: Degraded - working but suboptimal
- **Red**: Unreachable - needs attention
- **Purple**: Draining - intentional state
- **Gray**: Disabled - inactive

## Testing

```rust
#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use super::*;

    #[test]
    fn test_all_colors_parse() {
        // Ensure no color constant is malformed
        assert!(Color::parse(RchTheme::PRIMARY).is_ok());
        assert!(Color::parse(RchTheme::SECONDARY).is_ok());
        assert!(Color::parse(RchTheme::SUCCESS).is_ok());
        assert!(Color::parse(RchTheme::ERROR).is_ok());
        assert!(Color::parse(RchTheme::WARNING).is_ok());
        assert!(Color::parse(RchTheme::INFO).is_ok());
        assert!(Color::parse(RchTheme::STATUS_HEALTHY).is_ok());
        // ... etc for all constants
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_styles_dont_panic() {
        // Ensure style generators dont panic
        let _ = RchTheme::success();
        let _ = RchTheme::error();
        let _ = RchTheme::warning();
        let _ = RchTheme::info();
        let _ = RchTheme::worker_status("healthy");
        let _ = RchTheme::worker_status("unknown"); // Should not panic
    }
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

1. [ ] All color constants defined as valid hex colors
2. [ ] All style generator methods work without panic
3. [ ] worker_status() handles all WorkerStatus variants
4. [ ] worker_status() handles unknown strings gracefully
5. [ ] Colors visually distinct and accessible
6. [ ] Unit tests pass

 {"id":"bd-3u68","title":"Create RchConsole wrapper with context-aware output","description":"# Create RchConsole wrapper with context-aware output

## Task Description

Create rch/src/ui/console.rs with an RchConsole struct that wraps rich_rusts Console and automatically respects OutputContext. This is the primary interface for all rich output in RCH.

## Background

We need a wrapper because:
1. Raw Console doesnt know about our context detection
2. We need fallback methods for non-rich contexts
3. We want consistent behavior across all commands
4. We need to route output to stderr (not stdout)

## Implementation

```rust
// rch/src/ui/console.rs

use rich_rust::prelude::*;
use rch_common::ui::context::OutputContext;
use rch_common::ui::theme::RchTheme;
use std::io::{self, Write};

/// Context-aware console wrapper that respects output mode
///
/// This is the primary interface for rich output in RCH commands.
/// It automatically:
/// - Detects output context (interactive, piped, hook, etc.)
/// - Routes output to stderr (stdout reserved for data)
/// - Falls back to plain text when appropriate
/// - Respects NO_COLOR and FORCE_COLOR
pub struct RchConsole {
    /// The underlying rich_rust Console
    inner: Console,

    /// Detected output context
    context: OutputContext,
}

impl RchConsole {
    /// Create a new RchConsole with automatic context detection
    pub fn new() -> Self {
        let context = OutputContext::detect();
        let inner = Self::build_console(context);
        Self { inner, context }
    }

    /// Create with explicit context (for testing)
    pub fn with_context(context: OutputContext) -> Self {
        let inner = Self::build_console(context);
        Self { inner, context }
    }

    fn build_console(context: OutputContext) -> Console {
        Console::builder()
            .force_terminal(context.supports_rich())
            // Output to stderr by default for rich content
            // stdout is reserved for machine-readable data
            .build()
    }

    // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════
    // CONTEXT QUERIES
    // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════

    /// Check if rich output is available
    pub fn is_rich(&self) -> bool {
        self.context.supports_rich()
    }

    /// Check if colors are available
    pub fn is_colored(&self) -> bool {
        self.context.supports_color()
    }

    /// Check if in machine output mode
    pub fn is_machine(&self) -> bool {
        self.context.is_machine()
    }

    /// Get the detected context
    pub fn context(&self) -> OutputContext {
        self.context
    }

    /// Get terminal width (or default 80)
    pub fn width(&self) -> usize {
        self.inner.width()
    }

    // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════
    // RICH OUTPUT METHODS (only work in rich mode)
    // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════

    /// Print with markup parsing (only if interactive)
    ///
    /// Does nothing in machine/plain modes.
    pub fn print_rich(&self, content: &str) {
        if self.context.supports_rich() {
            self.inner.print(content);
        }
    }

    /// Print a renderable (table, panel, etc.)
    ///
    /// Does nothing in machine/plain modes.
    pub fn print_renderable<R: Renderable>(&self, renderable: &R) {
        if self.context.supports_rich() {
            self.inner.print_renderable(renderable);
        }
    }

    /// Print a horizontal rule
    ///
    /// Falls back to dashes in non-rich modes.
    pub fn rule(&self, title: Option<&str>) {
        if self.context.supports_rich() {
            self.inner.rule(title);
        } else if !self.context.is_machine() {
            // Plain fallback
            let width = 60;
            if let Some(t) = title {
                let padding = (width - t.len() - 2) / 2;
                eprintln!("{} {} {}", "-".repeat(padding), t, "-".repeat(padding));
            } else {
                eprintln!("{}", "-".repeat(width));
            }
        }
    }

    /// Print a blank line
    pub fn line(&self) {
        if !self.context.is_machine() {
            self.inner.line();
        }
    }

    // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════
    // FALLBACK OUTPUT METHODS
    // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════

    /// Print with rich/plain fallback
    ///
    /// In rich mode: prints rich content
    /// In plain mode: prints plain content
    /// In machine mode: prints nothing
    pub fn print_or_plain(&self, rich: &str, plain: &str) {
        if self.context.supports_rich() {
            self.inner.print(rich);
        } else if !self.context.is_machine() {
            eprintln!("{}", plain);
        }
    }

    /// Print plain text to stderr (always, unless machine mode)
    ///
    /// Use for messages that should appear in plain mode too.
    pub fn print_plain(&self, content: &str) {
        if !self.context.is_machine() {
            eprintln!("{}", content);
        }
    }

    /// Print error with rich panel or plain fallback
    ///
    /// Always prints (errors should show even in machine mode to stderr).
    pub fn print_error(&self, title: &str, message: &str) {
        if self.context.supports_rich() {
            let panel = Panel::from_text(message)
                .title(&format!("✗ {}", title))
                .border_style(RchTheme::error())
                .rounded();
            self.inner.print_renderable(&panel);
        } else {
            eprintln!("Error: {}", title);
            eprintln!("{}", message);
        }
    }

    /// Print success message
    pub fn print_success(&self, message: &str) {
        if self.context.supports_rich() {
            self.inner.print(&format!("[bold green]✓[/] {}", message));
        } else if !self.context.is_machine() {
            eprintln!("[OK] {}", message);
        }
    }

    /// Print warning message
    pub fn print_warning(&self, message: &str) {
        if self.context.supports_rich() {
            self.inner.print(&format!("[bold yellow]⚠[/] {}", message));
        } else if !self.context.is_machine() {
            eprintln!("[WARN] {}", message);
        }
    }

    /// Print info message
    pub fn print_info(&self, message: &str) {
        if self.context.supports_rich() {
            self.inner.print(&format!("[bold blue]ℹ[/] {}", message));
        } else if !self.context.is_machine() {
            eprintln!("[INFO] {}", message);
        }
    }

    // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════
    // MACHINE OUTPUT (to stdout)
    // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════

    /// Print JSON to stdout (for --json flag)
    ///
    /// This goes to stdout, not stderr!
    pub fn print_json<T: serde::Serialize>(&self, value: &T) -> serde_json::Result<()> {
        let json = serde_json::to_string_pretty(value)?;
        println!("{}", json);
        Ok(())
    }

    // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════
    // ACCESS TO UNDERLYING CONSOLE
    // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════

    /// Get the underlying rich_rust Console
    ///
    /// Use sparingly - prefer the wrapper methods.
    pub fn inner(&self) -> &Console {
        &self.inner
    }
}

impl Default for RchConsole {
    fn default() -> Self {
        Self::new()
    }
}

// ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════
// STATIC INSTANCE FOR CONVENIENCE
// ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════

use once_cell::sync::Lazy;

/// Global console instance with automatic context detection
///
/// Use this for quick access in command handlers:
/// ```
/// use rch::ui::console::CONSOLE;
/// CONSOLE.print_success("Done!");
/// ```
pub static CONSOLE: Lazy<RchConsole> = Lazy::new(RchConsole::new);
```

## Usage Examples

```rust
use rch::ui::console::{RchConsole, CONSOLE};

// Using global instance
fn handle_status_command() {
    CONSOLE.print_rich("[bold]Daemon Status[/]");
    // ...
}

// Using local instance with explicit context
fn handle_with_context(json_mode: bool) {
    let ctx = if json_mode {
        OutputContext::Machine
    } else {
        OutputContext::detect()
    };
    let console = RchConsole::with_context(ctx);

    if console.is_machine() {
        console.print_json(&status)?;
    } else {
        console.print_renderable(&status_table);
    }
}
```

#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use super::*;

    #[test]
    fn test_machine_mode_no_output() {
        let console = RchConsole::with_context(OutputContext::Machine);
        // These should do nothing (not panic)
        console.print_rich("[bold]test[/]");
        console.print_plain("test");
        // Only print_json and print_error should work
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_plain_mode_no_markup() {
        let console = RchConsole::with_context(OutputContext::Plain);
        // Should work but without markup
        console.print_plain("test");
        console.print_or_plain("[bold]rich[/]", "plain");
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_context_queries() {
        let interactive = RchConsole::with_context(OutputContext::Interactive);
        assert!(interactive.is_rich());
        assert!(interactive.is_colored());
        assert!(!interactive.is_machine());

        let machine = RchConsole::with_context(OutputContext::Machine);
        assert!(!machine.is_rich());
        assert!(machine.is_machine());
    }
}

 {"id":"bd-7ot6","title":"Create Test Fixture: Rust Project with Build Script (build.rs)","description":"## Purpose
Create a Rust project fixture with a custom build.rs build script for testing scenarios where build scripts generate code, link native libraries, or perform other build-time operations.

## Why This is Needed
Many real Rust projects use build.rs for:
- Code generation (protobuf, bindgen, etc.)
- Native library linking
- Environment variable configuration
- Conditional compilation

RCH must correctly handle projects where build.rs executes during compilation.

## Requirements
1. Project under tests/e2e/fixtures/with_build_rs/
2. Custom build.rs that generates a source file
3. Main code that uses the generated file
4. Verify generated code works on both local and remote

## Structure
```
tests/e2e/fixtures/with_build_rs/
├── Cargo.toml
├── build.rs          # Build script that generates code
└── src/
    └── main.rs       # Uses generated code via include!
```

Note: OUT_DIR is created by cargo during build, not committed.

## File Content Specifications

### Cargo.toml
```toml
[package]
name = "with_build_rs"
version = "0.1.0"
edition = "2021"
build = "build.rs"

[dependencies]
# No runtime dependencies needed for this simple fixture
```

### build.rs
```rust
//! Build script that generates version info and a greeting function.
//! This demonstrates common build.rs patterns used in real projects.

use std::env;
use std::fs;
use std::path::Path;

fn main() {
    // Get the output directory from cargo
    let out_dir = env::var("OUT_DIR").expect("OUT_DIR not set");
    let dest_path = Path::new(&out_dir).join("generated.rs");

    // Generate version info from environment
    let version = env::var("CARGO_PKG_VERSION").unwrap_or_else(|_| "unknown".to_string());
    let build_timestamp = chrono_like_timestamp();

    // Generate the Rust code
    let generated_code = format!(
        r#"// Auto-generated by build.rs - do not edit!

/// Returns version info generated at build time
pub fn version_info() -> &'static str {{
    "{version}"
}}

/// Returns the build timestamp
pub fn build_timestamp() -> &'static str {{
    "{build_timestamp}"
}}

/// A greeting generated at build time
pub fn generated_greeting() -> &'static str {{
    "Hello from build.rs generated code!"
}}

/// Demonstrates that build.rs ran successfully
pub const BUILD_RS_RAN: bool = true;
"#,
        version = version,
        build_timestamp = build_timestamp,
    );

    // Write the generated file
    fs::write(&dest_path, generated_code).expect("Failed to write generated.rs");

    // Tell cargo to rerun if build.rs changes
    println!("cargo:rerun-if-changed=build.rs");

    // Tell cargo this generates code
    println!("cargo:rerun-if-env-changed=CARGO_PKG_VERSION");
}

/// Simple timestamp without external dependencies
fn chrono_like_timestamp() -> String {
    // In a real project, you'd use chrono or time crate
    // For this fixture, we use a static string for reproducibility
    "2026-01-19T00:00:00Z".to_string()
}
```

### src/main.rs
```rust
//! Main binary that uses the build.rs generated code.
//! This demonstrates the include! pattern for generated modules.

// Include the generated code from OUT_DIR
include!(concat!(env!("OUT_DIR"), "/generated.rs"));

fn main() {
    println!("=== Build.rs Fixture Test ===");
    println!("Version: {}", version_info());
    println!("Build time: {}", build_timestamp());
    println!("Greeting: {}", generated_greeting());
    println!("build.rs ran: {}", BUILD_RS_RAN);
    println!("=== Test Complete ===");
}

## Test Cases This Enables

### 1. Build with build.rs
- Command: `cargo build`
- Verify: build.rs executes (check for generated.rs in target dir)
- Verify: Binary compiles successfully
- Verify: Binary produces expected output
- Log: build.rs execution, generated file creation

### 2. Remote build
- Sync project to worker
- Run `cargo build` on worker
- Verify: OUT_DIR is created on worker
- Verify: Generated code compiles on worker
- Verify: Artifact returns and runs locally
- Log: remote OUT_DIR location, artifact retrieval

### 3. Incremental build - change build.rs
- Modify build.rs (e.g., change greeting)
- Run `cargo build` again
- Verify: build.rs reruns (cargo:rerun-if-changed)
- Verify: New generated code is used
- Log: rebuild trigger, regeneration

### 4. Clean build cycle
- Run `cargo clean`
- Run `cargo build`
- Verify: Full generation cycle works
- Log: clean state, full regeneration

use std::process::Command;
use std::path::PathBuf;

#[test]
fn test_build_rs_fixture_compiles() {
    let fixture_path = PathBuf::from("tests/e2e/fixtures/with_build_rs");
    let output = Command::new("cargo")
        .args(["build"])
        .current_dir(&fixture_path)
        .output()
        .expect("cargo build failed");
    assert!(output.status.success(), "Build failed: {}", String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stderr));
}

#[test]
fn test_build_rs_generates_code() {
    let fixture_path = PathBuf::from("tests/e2e/fixtures/with_build_rs");
    // Build first
    let _ = Command::new("cargo")
        .args(["build"])
        .current_dir(&fixture_path)
        .output();

    // Check that generated.rs exists in OUT_DIR (inside target/debug/build/...)
    let build_dir = fixture_path.join("target/debug/build");
    assert!(build_dir.exists(), "Build directory should exist after build");

    // Find the generated file
    let has_generated = std::fs::read_dir(&build_dir)
        .unwrap()
        .filter_map(|e| e.ok())
        .any(|entry| {
            let path = entry.path();
            path.is_dir() && path.join("out/generated.rs").exists()
        });
    assert!(has_generated, "generated.rs should exist in OUT_DIR");
}

+//! True End-to-End Test Infrastructure
+//!
+//! This module provides infrastructure for running true E2E tests that
+//! execute against real worker machines over SSH.
+//!
+//! # Features
+//!
+//! This module is gated behind the `true-e2e` feature flag:
+//!
+//! ```bash
+//! cargo test --features true-e2e
+//! ```
+//!
+//! # Configuration
+//!
+//! Tests use the configuration in `tests/true_e2e/workers_test.toml`.
+//! Override with the `RCH_E2E_WORKERS_CONFIG` environment variable.
+//!
+//! # Example Usage
+//!
+//! ```rust,ignore
+//! use tests::true_e2e::config::TestWorkersConfig;
+//!
+//! #[test]
+//! fn test_with_real_worker() {
+//!     let config = TestWorkersConfig::load().expect("Failed to load config");
+//!
+//!     if !config.has_enabled_workers() {
+//!         eprintln!("Skipping: no workers configured");
+//!         return;
+//!     }
+//!
+//!     // Run test against real worker...
+//! }
+//! ```
+
+pub mod config;
+
+// Re-export common types for convenience
+pub use config::{ConfigError, ConfigResult, TestWorkersConfig, TestWorkerEntry, TestSettings};
+pub use config::{expand_path, get_config_path, should_skip_worker_check};
+pub use config::{ENV_WORKERS_CONFIG, ENV_SKIP_WORKER_CHECK, ENV_TIMEOUT_SECS};

+# RCH True E2E Test Worker Configuration
+#
+# This file configures real worker machines for true end-to-end testing.
+# Tests using this configuration will SSH to actual workers and execute builds.
+#
+# REQUIREMENTS FOR WORKERS:
+# - Passwordless SSH access via key authentication
+# - Rust nightly toolchain installed (matches rust-toolchain.toml)
+# - rsync available in PATH
+# - zstd available in PATH
+# - /tmp/rch_test writable directory
+#
+# USAGE:
+# 1. Copy this file or set RCH_E2E_WORKERS_CONFIG to override location
+# 2. Update worker entries with your actual worker details
+# 3. Run: cargo test --features true-e2e
+#
+# ENVIRONMENT VARIABLES:
+# - RCH_E2E_WORKERS_CONFIG: Override path to this config file
+# - RCH_E2E_SKIP_WORKER_CHECK: Skip worker availability check (for debugging)
+# - RCH_E2E_TIMEOUT_SECS: Override default command timeout (default: 300)
+#
+# CI NOTES:
+# - Tests will skip gracefully if no workers are reachable
+# - Set RCH_MOCK_SSH=1 to use mock SSH for CI (no real workers needed)
+
+# ==============================================================================
+# Test Settings
+# ==============================================================================
+
+[settings]
+# Default timeout for test commands in seconds
+default_timeout_secs = 300
+
+# SSH connection timeout in seconds
+ssh_connection_timeout_secs = 10
+
+# Remote working directory for test artifacts
+remote_work_dir = "/tmp/rch_test"
+
+# Rsync compression method
+rsync_compression = "zstd"
+
+# Whether to clean up remote artifacts after tests
+cleanup_after_test = true
+
+# Minimum required Rust version on workers (semver)
+min_rust_version = "1.85.0"
+
+# ==============================================================================
+# Worker Definitions
+# ==============================================================================
+# Add your worker machines below. At minimum, you need:
+# - id: Unique identifier (used in logs and selection)
+# - host: SSH hostname or IP address
+#
+# Optional fields with defaults:
+# - user: SSH username (default: "ubuntu")
+# - identity_file: Path to SSH key (default: "~/.ssh/id_rsa")
+# - total_slots: CPU slots available (default: 8)
+# - priority: Selection priority, higher = preferred (default: 100)
+# - tags: List of tags for filtering (default: [])
+# - enabled: Whether worker is active (default: true)
+
+# Example worker configuration (uncomment and modify for your environment):
+#
+# [[workers]]
+# id = "test-worker-1"
+# host = "192.168.1.100"
+# user = "ubuntu"
+# identity_file = "~/.ssh/id_rsa"
+# total_slots = 16
+# priority = 100
+# tags = ["rust", "test"]
+# enabled = true
+
+# ==============================================================================
+# Production Workers (Real Infrastructure)
+# ==============================================================================
+# These are the actual production workers. Uncomment if you have access.
+# IMPORTANT: These IPs are examples - replace with your actual worker IPs.
+
+# High-capacity bare metal server
+# [[workers]]
+# id = "css"
+# host = "209.145.54.164"
+# user = "ubuntu"
+# identity_file = "~/.ssh/contabo_new_baremetal_superserver_box.pem"
+# total_slots = 48
+# priority = 100
+# tags = ["baremetal", "high-capacity"]
+# enabled = true
+
+# Secondary bare metal server
+# [[workers]]
+# id = "csd"
+# host = "144.126.137.164"
+# user = "ubuntu"
+# identity_file = "~/.ssh/contabo_new_baremetal_sense_demo_box.pem"
+# total_slots = 48
+# priority = 100
+# tags = ["baremetal", "high-capacity"]
+# enabled = true
+
+# Medium-capacity VPS
+# [[workers]]
+# id = "fmd"
+# host = "51.222.245.56"
+# user = "ubuntu"
+# identity_file = "~/.ssh/je_ovh_ssh_key.pem"
+# total_slots = 16
+# priority = 90
+# tags = ["vps"]
+# enabled = true
+
+# Small VPS (lower priority)
+# [[workers]]
+# id = "yto"
+# host = "37.187.75.150"
+# user = "ubuntu"
+# identity_file = "~/.ssh/je_ovh_ssh_key.pem"
+# total_slots = 8
+# priority = 80
+# tags = ["vps", "small"]
+# enabled = true
